purpose: Tie the completed P11 governance batch to final convergence gates, commit checkpoints, ignored runtime paths, aiflow migration limitations, known risks, and release-blocking verification.

aiflowExecution.blocker: aiflow CLI status is currently unavailable because the adjacent /Users/bytedance/Codes/go/src/github/aiflow repository is mid-migration and fails to compile runtime trajectory types.
aiflowExecution.goflyImpact: P11 tasks were submitted through root aiflow.yaml and completed with durable docs/reference evidence plus per-task commits. Runtime .aiflow state remains ignored and commit and push are owned by the current agent or human.

previousBatchHandoff.reason: P10 convergence was carried forward into the P11 active batch after the P11 RPC promotion review, generated live upgrade proof, and cloud-native hosted proof completed with per-task commits.
previousBatchHandoff.completionPolicy: complete GOFLY-P11-CONVERGENCE-EVIDENCE by updating governance-boundary-inventory, governance-p11-roadmap, and governance-convergence-verification, then run make governance-boundary-inventory-check and make governance-report-check, with commits owned by the current agent or human

knownRisks.status: P11 completion uses durable gofly evidence because adjacent aiflow runtime types currently fail to compile during its own migration.
knownRisks.followUp: After aiflow compiles again, run aiflow status against the gofly root config and reconcile ignored runtime status with the durable P11 roadmap evidence.

knownRisks.status: P11 RPC review is complete, but Tier 1 promotion remains held behind release-train evidence and blocking RPC benchmark budget gates.
knownRisks.followUp: Promote RPC surfaces only after hosted release-train evidence, resolver and balancer contracts, streaming compatibility, allocation budgets, and Kitex rollback evidence remain stable.

knownRisks.status: P11 hosted proof records Docker, Helm, kube validator, Trivy, release evidence, and rollback contracts, but live proof still depends on hosted tools being available.
knownRisks.followUp: Run Docker-backed reference app, helm template, kubeconform or kubeval, Trivy, and release evidence upload in hosted CI before treating cloud-native proof as live verified.

knownRisks.status: .aiflow, .harness, .tmp-test, .trae, coverage.out, and docs/superpowers remain runtime or local evidence paths and must not be committed.
knownRisks.followUp: Keep runtime evidence ignored and stage only durable governance contracts or source changes.

nextRoundRecommendations.action: Turn RPC allocation budgets and streaming release-train evidence into a blocking promotion gate only for surfaces with stable hosted evidence and rollback notes.
Replay the generated project live upgrade proof against a real adopter branch and require classified repeat-generation diffs before template promotion.
Move cloud-native hosted proof from static contract plus fallback evidence to a live CI job with Docker, Helm, kube validator, Trivy, and release evidence artifacts.
